channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Federal workers confront mass confusion as Musk's deadline to list accomplishments looms
item.description: Confusion and chaos loom as hundreds of thousands of federal employees begin their workweek on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Steve Peoples, Eric Tucker And Amanda Seitz, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Millionaire Vivek Ramaswamy set to join the Ohio governor's race
item.description: A Republican who left the Department of Government Efficiency initiative on President Donald Trump’s first day is expected to launch his bid for Ohio governor.
item.dc:creator: Julie Carr Smyth, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The Trump administration is putting USAID staffers on leave worldwide and firing at least 1,600
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration moved its fast-paced dismantling of the U.S. Agency for International Development toward what appeared to be its final phases, telling all but a fraction of staffers worldwide that they were on leave as of Monday and notifying at least 1,600 of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US pressures Ukraine to nix its UN resolution demanding Russian forces withdraw
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The U.S. has pressured Ukraine to withdraw its European-backed U.N. resolution demanding an immediate withdrawal of Russian forces from Ukraine in favor of an American proposal that does not mention Moscow’s invasion, a U.S. official and a European diplomat said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-Secret Service agent and conservative media personality Dan Bongino picked as FBI deputy director
item.description: Dan Bongino, a former U.S. Secret Service agent who has penned best-selling books, ran unsuccessfully for office and gained fame as a conservative pundit with TV shows and a popular podcast, has been chosen to serve as FBI deputy director.
item.dc:creator: Adriana Gomez Licon And Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 80 years ago Marines raised flag over Iwo Jima in iconic moment
item.description: On this day in 1945, six U.S. Marines raised the American flag over the island of Iwo Jima on the fourth day of what would become over a monthlong brutal battle.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump celebrates conservative party win in Germany
item.description: President Donald Trump praised German conservative opposition leader Friedrich Merz's victory in the national election on Sunday, ousting current Chancellor Olaf Scholz.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democratic governors balance whether to fight or pacify after Trump threatens one of their own
item.description: President Donald Trump’s real-time confrontation with Maine’s governor over transgender athletes captured the conundrum many Democratic governors are facing in the Republican’s second term.
item.dc:creator: Joey Cappelletti, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump set to meet with world leaders in DC during busy week as Russia-Ukraine peace talks continue
item.description: President Trump will meet with the French president and U.K. prime minister this week as administration leaders continue efforts to reach a peace deal between Russia and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DOD tells civilian workforce to ignore Elon Musk's request to report productivity
item.description: Pentagon officials told civilian employees to pause responses to the Department of Government Efficiency's (DOGE) request to report five things they did last week to keep their jobs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas A&M regents may soon decide the university system’s next leader
item.description: Sources indicate the board has narrowed its search to five candidates: Texas Comptroller Glenn Hegar, U.S. Rep. Michael McCaul, R-Austin, Texas A&M Foundation President Tyson Voelkel, University of Alabama President Stuart Bell and state Rep. Trent Ashby, R-Lufkin.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Kate Mcgee And Jessica Priest, KPRC 2

item.title: Zelenskyy offers to resign in exchange for peace or Ukraine’s NATO membership
item.description: “If it’s about peace in Ukraine, and you really want me to leave my position – I am ready to do that," Zelenskyy told reporters at a press conference in Kyiv.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth defends Trump's firings of Pentagon leaders and says there may be more dismissals
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth insists President Donald Trump ’s abrupt firing of the nation’s senior military officer amid a wave of dismissals at the Pentagon wasn't unusual, brushing aside outcry that the new administration is openly seeking to inject politics into the military.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Key federal agencies refuse to comply with Musk's latest demand in his cost-cutting crusade
item.description: Key federal agencies such as the FBI, State Department and Pentagon have instructed their employees not to comply with Elon Musk’s latest demand that they explain what they accomplished last week or risk losing their job.
item.dc:creator: Steve Peoples, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hochul slams Trump’s ‘king’ remark after president's move to block congestion pricing toll in NYC
item.description: New York Gov. Kathy Hochul is pushing back against President Donald Trump’s “King" remark after his administration moved to block the state’s congestion pricing program.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says he'd be ready to give up presidency if it brought peace, NATO membership
item.description: “If to achieve peace, you really need me to give up my post, I’m ready,” Zelenskyy said in response to a journalist's question on whether he’d trade his office for peace. “I can trade it for NATO.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DHS will release some of Prince Harry's immigration docs amid allegations he lied about drug use
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) will release parts of redacted documents related to the immigration case of Prince Harry to a federal court by March 6.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Veterans ask Trump to award Medal of Honor to Marine Corps war hero snubbed by Biden
item.description: A group of veterans and lawmakers is urging President Donald Trump to award an 87-year-old Marine Corps "legend" and war hero the Medal of Honor after he was snubbed by former President Joe Biden.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Following key wins, Trump poised for cabinet completion in record time
item.description: After successfully confirming his most controversial picks for top Cabinet roles, the Senate is on track to push them all past the finish line—and in record time.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Many Americans don't trust the media to cover Trump: Survey
item.description: Most Americans in a new survey said they have little confidence that the news media can cover President Trump with fairness and accuracy.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: A look at Dan 'Razin' Caine, Trump's pick to be the top US military officer
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s choice to be the top U.S. military officer, retired Air Force Lt. Gen. Dan Caine, endeared himself to the commander in chief through his military call sign, “Razin,” during a meeting in Iraq years ago.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: National Park Service rangers laid off as DOGE makes cuts
item.description: National Park Service ranger Mikayla Moors was one of thousands of government employees laid off by the DOGE.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Judge largely blocks Trump‘s executive orders ending federal support for DEI programs
item.description: A U.S. district judge granted a preliminary injunction blocking the administration from terminating or changing federal contracts they consider equity-related.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Elon Musk blurs the line between his government and business roles
item.description: The tech titan and President Trump say they will avoid any conflicts of interest, but it’s difficult for the public to verify that.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith, MPR News

item.title: PIX Panel discusses Hochul’s safeguard plan for NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Gov. Kathy Hochul on Thursday announced proposed safeguards to ensure that New York City is operating in the best interest of its residents.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: The Supreme Court won’t allow Trump to immediately fire head of whistleblower office
item.description: The Supreme Court on Friday temporarily kept on the job the head of the federal agency that protects government whistleblowers, in its first word on the many legal fights over President Donald Trump’s second-term agenda.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Pentagon says it will cut 5,400 probationary workers starting next week
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Defense Department said Friday that it’s cutting 5,400 probationary workers starting next week and will put a hiring freeze in place.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US Marshals Service deputizes Musk's security team: Reports
item.description: The U.S. Marshals Service has deputized some of tech billionaire Elon Musk's private security detail, giving them specific rights and protections of federal law enforcement agents, multiple outlets reported.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: Judge cancels trial for NYC Mayor Eric Adams but leaves corruption charges intact for now
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge on Friday cancelled the corruption trial for New York City Mayor Eric Adams and appointed counsel to advise him on how to handle the Justice Department’s controversial request to drop charges against the Democrat. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: 5 takeaways from Elon Musk's CPAC talk
item.description: Elon Musk served as one of the main attractions at the Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) with a late-scheduled appearance that saw him wield a chainsaw.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Lawmakers to Trump: Reverse cuts to 9/11 health care
item.description: New York's senate and congressional delegations agree that federal cuts to the World Trade Center Health Program will hurt survivors of and first responders to the Sept. 11 terrorist attacks.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, PIX 11

item.title: Judge rules Trump administration can move ahead on mass government layoffs
item.description: A federal judge Thursday refused a group of government employee unions’ request to block the Trump administration from moving ahead with plans to dramatically reduce the federal workforce. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Bill that would make more attorney general data public fails in Minnesota House vote
item.description: House Republicans pushed for a vote on a bill to require the attorney general’s office, occupied now by DFLer Keith Ellison, to be more forthcoming with records. Democrats framed it as a partisan measure.
item.dc:creator: Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: Ellison: Trump order on transgender athletes violates Minnesota law
item.description: Attorney General Keith Ellison’s opinion comes days after the Trump administration said it would investigate the state high school league’s plan to follow state law and let students participate in sports consistent with their gender identity, despite the president’s executive order.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Shockman and Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: USDA to follow ‘president’s lead’ on foreign farm ownership policy
item.description: The concern, however, is that international ownership could lead to risks of spying or economic pressure.
item.dc:creator: Alex Caprariello, NewsNation

item.title: Former NFL player Chris Kluwe arrested while protesting MAGA sign at city council meeting
item.description: Former NFL punter Chris Kluwe was detained at a city council meeting in California on Tuesday after delivering an impassioned speech criticizing the design of a plaque to be installed at the Huntington Beach Central Library.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Frey vetoes council move toward a pedestrian plaza at George Floyd Square
item.description: Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey has vetoed the City Council’s plan to explore building a pedestrian-only mall at George Floyd Square, the intersection where an officer killed Floyd in 2020.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News
